status_detail: AEM (Anion Exchange Membrane) electrolysis production facility in Pisa, Italy. Enapter's multi-core AEM Electrolyser produces green hydrogen at 35 bar without precious metals. Operational since 2022. AEM technology bridges PEM and Alkaline — using anion-exchange membrane with nickel-based catalysts. Scalable modular architecture (EL 2.1 and EL 4.0 platforms).

data_management.narrative_summary: Enapter is an Italian-German company producing AEM (Anion Exchange Membrane) electrolysers at its facility in Pisa, Italy. AEM electrolysis combines the advantages of PEM (compact design, fast dynamics, differential pressure) with those of Alkaline (no precious metal catalysts, low-cost materials). Enapter's multi-core AEM Electrolyser operates without iridium or platinum, using nickel-based catalysts and an anion-exchange membrane. The modular architecture allows scaling from single-core (0.5 Nm3/h) to multi-MW installations. The e-RINA project demonstrated AEM technology at pilot scale. Enapter also operates a major production facility in Saerbeck, Germany. This project represents the most commercially advanced AEM electrolysis deployment globally.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected with solar PV integration at Enapter's Pisa facility

offtake.offtaker_name: On-site industrial and R&D use, demonstration customers

sources.title: Enapter AEM electrolyser — e-RINA project and commercial production launch
